next_action: Spawn gsd-research-synthesizer agent per new-milestone.md workflow step 8, reading the 4 research files, writing .planning/research/SUMMARY.md using template $HOME/.claude/gsd-core/templates/research-project/SUMMARY.md, then committing. Apply the synthesizer output self-heal check (#222) from the workflow before trusting SUMMARY.md exists.
context_notes: This is a /gsd-new-milestone run for Janus 2.0, paused via explicit user /gsd-pause-work request right after 2 of 4 parallel researcher agents hit the Claude session limit. No code was touched this session — this is pure planning-doc work (PROJECT.md, STATE.md, research/*.md). The research findings are strong: STACK.md confirms zero new dependencies needed (third instantiation of the proven v2.1/v2.2 pattern); FEATURES.md flags that real Exchange full-access does NOT imply send-as (v2.3's simplified level ladder should be logged as an explicit Key Decision, not presented as faithfully modeling Exchange); ARCHITECTURE.md gives a concrete 3-phase build order (13=model/engine, 14=seed/state/selectors, 15=UI) with exact file/line anchors into the existing v2.2 demo code; PITFALLS.md lists 9 concrete pitfalls mapped to which phase should catch each one, most importantly reiterating the project's 'defer features, not enforcement' and 'dead trace row' (advisory zone-prereq shipped as dead code, caught only by live UAT) lessons from v2.2 as directly applicable to the new dataset gate-chain.
